question.content: For the elastic collision setup, determine the post-collision angular velocity omega_1 of the first body under the stated rightward-positive convention. Output exactly one expression token sequence in plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: a single algebraic expression for the target quantity. Use the variable names from the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Method: contact-point restitution together with angular impulse about the two suspension points.
step_1.key_point: Use the same contact impulse J for both bodies, then replace the energy equation by elastic reversal of the contact-point relative velocity.

step_3.content: Take angular impulse about O1 and O2, so the unknown support reactions never enter. The single impact impulse J immediately gives post-impact angular speeds of both bodies.
step_3.key_point: Moments about the suspension points eliminate all hinge impulses at once.

step_5.content: Because the collision is elastic, the normal relative speed at the contact point reverses. Write that contact-point velocity condition with the two post-impact angular speeds instead of introducing a separate kinetic-energy equation.
step_5.key_point: The restitution condition replaces the energy-conservation route used in solution1.

step_7.content: Solve the angular-impulse relations together with the restitution relation, then apply the rightward-positive convention. The first body turns opposite to the positive direction, so omega_1 is negative.
step_7.key_point: The sign comes from the final rotation sense of body 1 under the stated convention.
